Title: Hero Character Sheet: A Fantasy Protagonist Built Like Game Concept Art
Teaser: Turns one character idea into a full concept-art portrait prompt with named lighting, materials and a believable backstory read in the pose.
PromptHint: Describe the [CHARACTER] in plain terms; set a [WORLD/ERA] and an optional [MOOD].
Prompt: You are a senior character concept artist at a AAA game studio who writes image prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Turn my idea into ONE cinematic, photoreal-painted character key art. Character: [CHARACTER]. World/era: [WORLD/ERA]. Mood: [MOOD]. THINK FIRST (silently, never shown): who this person is, what their story tells through pose and wear, the single light that gives them presence, what materials (worn leather, etched steel, frayed cloth) sell the world. Then write ONE copy-paste image description in flowing natural sentences covering, in order: the character's face + expression + apparent age, body and pose, armor/clothing with named materials and signs of use, props/weapon, environment hinted behind them, lighting (name the source + color temperature, e.g. cold rim light 6500K against a warm fire key), camera (e.g. waist-up, 50mm, shallow depth), art direction (painterly concept art, Craig Mullins / Feng Zhu grade), and mood. HARD RULES: anatomically correct hands, no extra limbs, no text or watermarks, no generic 'fantasy hero' cliche armor. End with a Midjourney param line: --ar 2:3 --v 6 --style raw --s 250. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <the full description + param line> 2) THREE VARIANT SWAPS: <three single-phrase swaps that re-class the character, e.g. rogue / paladin / outcast>. Nothing else.

Title: Sci-Fi Megacity: An Establishing Shot From a Film That Doesn't Exist
Teaser: Builds a wide cinematic sci-fi city establishing shot with named architecture language, atmosphere and scale cues so it reads as a real frame.
PromptHint: Name the [CITY CONCEPT]; set a [TIME OF DAY/WEATHER] and an optional [TECH ERA].
Prompt: You are a film concept artist designing establishing shots for a sci-fi feature, writing prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Build ONE wide, cinematic establishing shot of a sci-fi city. City concept: [CITY CONCEPT]. Time of day / weather: [TIME OF DAY/WEATHER]. Tech era: [TECH ERA]. THINK FIRST (silently): the one silhouette that defines the skyline, how scale is communicated (tiny vehicles, human figures, layered depth), the atmosphere that adds mood, and the single light source that anchors the frame. Then write ONE copy-paste image description in flowing sentences covering, in order: the dominant architecture and its design language (brutalist, organic-grown, art-deco-retrofuture, name one), city layers and verticality, signs of life (traffic skylanes, holographic signage, crowds), atmosphere (haze, rain, neon bloom, volumetric fog), lighting (key source + color temp, motivated practicals), camera (wide aerial or low hero angle, lens, deep focus), art direction (matte-painting realism, Syd Mead / Blade Runner lineage), and mood. HARD RULES: coherent perspective and consistent light, no warped or gibberish signage text, no melted geometry. End with a Midjourney param line: --ar 21:9 --v 6 --style raw --s 300. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <the full description + param line> 2) MOOD SWAPS: <two single-phrase swaps that flip the city from utopian to dystopian>. Nothing else.

Title: Original Creature Design: A Believable Beast With Real Biology
Teaser: Designs a never-seen-before fantasy or alien creature grounded in plausible anatomy, habitat and behavior, not a random monster mash.
PromptHint: Give a [CREATURE SEED] (size, vibe, or two animals to blend); name its [HABITAT].
Prompt: You are a creature concept designer for film and games who writes image prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Design ONE original, believable creature. Creature seed: [CREATURE SEED]. Habitat: [HABITAT]. THINK FIRST (silently): what this creature eats and how it moves, which real anatomy makes it convincing (musculature, skin/scale/feather texture, eye placement for predator vs prey), how the habitat shaped its form, and the one feature that makes it memorable. Then write ONE copy-paste image description in flowing sentences covering, in order: overall body plan and size relative to a known reference, named surface texture and coloration with a purpose (camouflage, warning display), distinctive features (horns, bioluminescence, limb count) and why they exist, pose showing natural behavior, the habitat around it, lighting (source + color temp that flatters the texture), camera (three-quarter view, lens, depth), art direction (natural-history-illustration realism meets concept art), and mood. HARD RULES: consistent anatomy (count limbs, no floating parts), no existing copyrighted monster, no text. End with a Midjourney param line: --ar 4:3 --v 6 --style raw --s 250 --c 10. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <the full description + param line> 2) BIOLOGY NOTE: <one line on its diet, movement and the function of its standout feature> 3) WILD CARD: <one swap that makes it stranger but still plausible>. Nothing else.

Title: Epic Fantasy Landscape: A Vista Worth a Two-Page Spread
Teaser: Crafts a sweeping fantasy landscape prompt with named atmospheric perspective, a focal landmark and human-scale cues so the world feels vast and real.
PromptHint: Describe the [LANDSCAPE/LANDMARK]; set a [SEASON/TIME] and an optional [MYTH FEEL].
Prompt: You are a matte-painting landscape artist for fantasy films, writing prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Paint ONE breathtaking, vast fantasy vista. Landscape / landmark: [LANDSCAPE/LANDMARK]. Season / time: [SEASON/TIME]. Myth feel: [MYTH FEEL]. THINK FIRST (silently): the single focal landmark the eye lands on, how depth is built in three planes (foreground detail, mid-ground subject, hazy background), the human or creature figure that gives true scale, and the light that makes it feel mythic. Then write ONE copy-paste image description in flowing sentences covering, in order: a foreground detail to anchor scale, the mid-ground focal landmark, layered receding background with atmospheric perspective (cooler and lighter with distance), a tiny figure or caravan for scale, weather and atmosphere (mist, god rays, drifting snow), lighting (golden hour, blue hour, or storm break with color temp), camera (wide vista, lens, deep focus), art direction (epic matte painting, Ted Nasmith / Studio Ghibli backdrop grade), and mood. HARD RULES: believable geology and consistent light direction, no repeating tiled textures, no text. End with a Midjourney param line: --ar 16:9 --v 6 --style raw --s 300. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <the full description + param line> 2) TIME SWAPS: <two single-phrase swaps that change the hour and weather>. Nothing else.

Title: Mech / Vehicle Design: Hard-Surface Concept With Real Engineering Logic
Teaser: Generates a hard-surface mech or vehicle prompt with named panel lines, function-first design and weathering so it looks engineered, not doodled.
PromptHint: Name the [MACHINE TYPE] and its [JOB/ROLE]; add an optional [FACTION LOOK].
Prompt: You are a hard-surface vehicle and mech concept designer who writes image prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Design ONE believable sci-fi machine. Machine type: [MACHINE TYPE]. Job / role: [JOB/ROLE]. Faction look: [FACTION LOOK]. THINK FIRST (silently): what this machine's job demands of its shape (cargo bays, weapon mounts, cockpit visibility, ground clearance), where stress and heat would force thick armor or vents, the silhouette that reads instantly, and the weathering that proves it has worked. Then write ONE copy-paste image description in flowing sentences covering, in order: the overall silhouette and how it serves the role, panel lines and mechanical detail (rivets, hydraulics, exposed cabling, vents), named materials (matte ceramic armor, brushed titanium, scorched alloy), faction color scheme and markings, weathering (grime streaks, paint chips, battle scars), pose or staging in a fitting environment, lighting (rim light to define the silhouette + color temp), camera (three-quarter hero angle, lens, sharp focus), art direction (industrial concept art, orthographic-grade clarity), and mood. HARD RULES: consistent mechanical logic and symmetry where intended, no warped geometry, no gibberish text on decals. End with a Midjourney param line: --ar 16:9 --v 6 --style raw --s 200. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <the full description + param line> 2) DESIGN NOTE: <one line on how the silhouette communicates its function> 3) ROLE SWAP: <one phrase that re-purposes the machine for a different job>. Nothing else.

Title: Key Art Splash: A Game Cover Frame That Sells the Whole World
Teaser: Composes a hero key-art splash prompt with a clear focal character, dynamic composition and copy space for a title so it reads like a cover.
PromptHint: Describe the [HERO + STAKES]; name the [WORLD] and an optional [TITLE-SPACE SIDE].
Prompt: You are an art director producing key art splashes for game covers and posters, writing prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Compose ONE dramatic hero key-art frame. Hero + stakes: [HERO + STAKES]. World: [WORLD]. Title-space side: [TITLE-SPACE SIDE]. THINK FIRST (silently): the single emotional beat the cover must sell, the reading order (hero first, then threat, then world), the dynamic diagonal that adds energy, and where the logo and title will sit. Then write ONE copy-paste image description in flowing sentences covering, in order: the focal hero in a charged pose with clear emotion, the central conflict or threat behind them, the world environment framing the moment, depth and atmosphere (particles, embers, dust), a dynamic composition with a strong diagonal and clear focal hierarchy, deliberate negative space on the [TITLE-SPACE SIDE] for a logo, lighting (dramatic key + rim, named color temp, strong contrast), camera (low heroic angle, lens), art direction (epic poster illustration, painterly realism), and mood. HARD RULES: keep the title side clean, no actual text or logos in the image, anatomically correct hero, no clutter that breaks focal flow. End with a Midjourney param line: --ar 3:4 --v 6 --style raw --s 250. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <the full description + param line> 2) LAYOUT NOTE: <one line on where the logo, title and tagline go>. Nothing else.

Title: Faction Banner & Heraldry: A Coherent Visual Identity for a Made-Up Kingdom
Teaser: Designs a fantasy faction's emblem, banner and color story as one consistent identity prompt set, with symbolism that maps to the lore.
PromptHint: Describe the [FACTION] and its [VALUES/LORE]; add an optional [CULTURE INSPIRATION].
Prompt: You are a world-building art director who designs faction identities and writes image prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Design ONE coherent heraldic identity for an invented faction. Faction: [FACTION]. Values / lore: [VALUES/LORE]. Culture inspiration: [CULTURE INSPIRATION]. THINK FIRST (silently): which symbol best encodes the faction's values, the two- or three-color palette that signals its mood (and what each color means), the material the banner is made of, and how the emblem stays legible at a glance. Deliver in this exact OUTPUT FORMAT: 1) IDENTITY DNA: one plain-text line fixing the central emblem and its symbolism, the named color palette with the meaning of each color, the heraldic style (ban 'cool', 'epic' as descriptors), and the banner material. 2) THREE IMAGE PROMPTS that each PREPEND the Identity DNA verbatim, then add: (a) a clean centered emblem on a neutral field, (b) a weathered cloth banner hanging in a fitting environment with named lighting + color temp, and (c) the emblem applied to an object (shield, sail, armor plate) with realistic material and wear; each as flowing natural sentences ending with a Midjourney param line --ar 1:1 --v 6 --style raw --s 180 for (a), --ar 2:3 for (b), --ar 4:3 for (c). 3) CONSISTENCY NOTE: one line on keeping the emblem shape and palette identical across all three. RULE: the Identity DNA wording must be byte-for-byte identical in every prompt; no real-world or copyrighted heraldry. Nothing else.

Title: Bioluminescent Alien Biome: An Otherworldly Environment That Obeys Its Own Physics
Teaser: Builds an alien-world environment prompt with invented-but-consistent flora, light sources and atmosphere so the strangeness feels lived-in and real.
PromptHint: Name the [BIOME TYPE] and its [DOMINANT LIFE]; set an optional [ATMOSPHERE/COLOR].
Prompt: You are an environment concept artist designing alien biospheres, writing prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Build ONE immersive, otherworldly environment that feels internally consistent. Biome type: [BIOME TYPE]. Dominant life: [DOMINANT LIFE]. Atmosphere / color: [ATMOSPHERE/COLOR]. THINK FIRST (silently): the rules of this world (what glows, what the light source is, what the gravity or air implies for plant shapes), the dominant lifeform and how it shapes the scene, the layered depth, and the one detail that proves it's alien yet plausible. Then write ONE copy-paste image description in flowing sentences covering, in order: the foreground invented flora or formations with named texture, the mid-ground dominant life or structure, layered receding background, the light sources (bioluminescent spores, twin suns, glowing minerals) with named color temps, atmosphere (mist, floating particles, refracted haze), a small known-scale element for grounding, camera (wide immersive angle, lens, depth), art direction (sci-fi environment concept art, painterly realism with hard physical logic), and mood. HARD RULES: consistent light logic, no melted geometry, no Earth plants pasted in, no text. End with a Midjourney param line: --ar 16:9 --v 6 --style raw --s 300 --c 15. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <the full description + param line> 2) WORLD RULE: <one line stating the physical rule that makes the scene cohere> 3) PALETTE SWAP: <one phrase that recolors the whole biome>. Nothing else.

Title: Style Lock for a Series: One Reusable Concept-Art Look Across Every Asset
Teaser: Defines a single repeatable art-direction language for a project, then outputs three on-style scene prompts that share one look and palette.
PromptHint: Describe the [PROJECT/WORLD] and its [STYLE GOAL]; name three [ASSETS] you need on-style.
Prompt: You are an art director locking the visual language for a fantasy or sci-fi project, writing prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Project / world: [PROJECT/WORLD]. Style goal: [STYLE GOAL]. Assets I need on-style: [ASSETS]. THINK FIRST (silently): which visual traits MUST stay fixed across every asset (palette, brushwork, light character, level of detail, mood) versus what changes per asset, so the whole series looks like one hand made it. Deliver in this exact OUTPUT FORMAT: 1) STYLE DNA: one plain-text line fixing the named palette (with concrete colors), the rendering style (painterly vs crisp), the lighting character, the texture/grain, and the overall mood in concrete words (ban 'beautiful', 'epic', 'amazing'). 2) THREE ASSET PROMPTS: for each asset, one copy-paste image description in flowing sentences that PREPENDS the Style DNA verbatim, then adds the asset-specific subject, environment, lighting with color temp, and camera, each ending with a Midjourney param line --ar 16:9 --v 6 --style raw --s 200 --sref [PASTE YOUR REFERENCE IMAGE URL HERE]. 3) CONSISTENCY NOTE: one line on reusing the same --sref and tuning --sw for tighter or looser adherence. RULE: the Style DNA wording must be byte-for-byte identical in every asset prompt. Nothing else.

Title: Spell & Magic FX: VFX-Grade Energy That Reads as Real Light
Teaser: Designs a spellcasting or energy-effect prompt with named particle behavior, light interaction and color logic so the magic looks like real VFX, not a glow filter.
PromptHint: Describe the [CASTER/SOURCE] and the [SPELL EFFECT]; set an optional [ELEMENT/COLOR].
Prompt: You are a VFX concept artist who designs magic and energy effects, writing prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Render ONE believable spellcasting moment where the magic behaves like real light. Caster / source: [CASTER/SOURCE]. Spell effect: [SPELL EFFECT]. Element / color: [ELEMENT/COLOR]. THINK FIRST (silently): how this energy moves (swirling, arcing, erupting), how it lights the caster and the environment (cast light, colored bounce, rim glow), the particle and density behavior, and the one beat of the spell to freeze for maximum drama. Then write ONE copy-paste image description in flowing sentences covering, in order: the caster's pose and focused expression, the energy's shape and motion described physically, how the light from the spell illuminates the caster's face and the surroundings (named color temp and bounce), particle and atmosphere behavior (sparks, embers, vapor, distortion), the environment reacting (wind, debris, shadows thrown), camera (dynamic angle, lens, motion-implying framing), art direction (cinematic VFX realism, volumetric lighting), and mood. HARD RULES: the magic must cast realistic colored light on everything near it, anatomically correct caster, no flat sticker-glow, no text. End with a Midjourney param line: --ar 16:9 --v 6 --style raw --s 300. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <the full description + param line> 2) FX NOTE: <one line on how the spell light interacts with the scene> 3) ELEMENT SWAP: <one phrase that changes the element and recolors the light>. Nothing else.

Title: Same Character, Many Scenes: Lock the Face and Outfit Across a Whole Story
Teaser: Builds a reusable character identity block plus three scene prompts that keep the same face, build and signature gear across different settings.
PromptHint: Describe the [CHARACTER] once; name three [SCENES] to drop them into.
Prompt: You are a character designer building a reusable hero for a fantasy or sci-fi series, writing prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Character: [CHARACTER]. Scenes: [SCENES]. THINK FIRST (silently): which traits MUST stay fixed across every render (facial structure, apparent age, skin tone, eye color, hairstyle, body type, signature wardrobe and props) versus what changes per scene. Deliver in this exact OUTPUT FORMAT: 1) LOCKED IDENTITY BLOCK: one plain-text line of concrete descriptors fixing the character's face, age, skin tone, eyes, hair, build, and signature outfit and gear (ban 'pretty', 'cool', 'epic'). 2) THREE SCENE PROMPTS: for each scene, one copy-paste image description in flowing sentences that PREPENDS the Locked Identity Block verbatim, then adds the scene-specific environment, the character's action and pose, lighting with named source and color temp, and camera, each ending with a Midjourney param line --ar 2:3 --v 7 --style raw --s 200 --oref [PASTE CHARACTER IMAGE URL] --ow 200. 3) CONSISTENCY NOTE: one line on lowering --ow when the face fights the scene, and that --oref is Midjourney v7 only (use --cref for v6). RULE: the identity wording must be byte-for-byte identical in every scene. Nothing else.

Title: Lived-In Prop & Artifact: A Single Object That Hints at a Whole World
Teaser: Designs one storytelling prop or artifact prompt with named materials, age and wear so a single object implies the culture and history behind it.
PromptHint: Name the [ARTIFACT] and its [PURPOSE/LORE]; add an optional [CULTURE/ERA].
Prompt: You are a prop and artifact concept designer who writes image prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Design ONE storytelling object that implies an entire world. Artifact: [ARTIFACT]. Purpose / lore: [PURPOSE/LORE]. Culture / era: [CULTURE/ERA]. THINK FIRST (silently): what this object was made for and by whom, the materials and craftsmanship that reveal its culture, the wear and repairs that prove it has been used and loved or fought over, and the single detail that hooks curiosity. Then write ONE copy-paste image description in flowing sentences covering, in order: the object's overall form and what it does, named materials and craftsmanship (carved bone, hammered brass, glowing inlay, frayed binding), ornamentation and motifs tied to its culture, age and wear (patina, chips, hairline cracks, field repairs), how it is staged (resting on a surface, held, half-buried) with a fitting backdrop, lighting (museum-style key + soft fill, or moody single source, with color temp) that flatters the materials, camera (close product-style three-quarter angle, macro lens, shallow depth), art direction (concept-art realism, tactile detail), and mood. HARD RULES: physically plausible construction, consistent material logic, no gibberish runes-as-text unless described as decorative marks, no watermark. End with a Midjourney param line: --ar 4:5 --v 6 --style raw --s 220. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <the full description + param line> 2) LORE HOOK: <one line on the story this object's wear and details imply>. Nothing else.

Title: Boss Encounter: A Cinematic Showdown Staged Like a Game Set-Piece
Teaser: Composes a hero-versus-boss confrontation prompt with clear scale, staging, focal flow and atmosphere so the standoff reads as an epic set-piece moment.
PromptHint: Describe the [HERO] and the [BOSS/THREAT]; set the [ARENA] and an optional [MOOD].
Prompt: You are a cinematic concept artist staging boss encounters for games, writing prompts for GPT-4o / Gemini and Midjourney. Compose ONE dramatic hero-versus-boss confrontation. Hero: [HERO]. Boss / threat: [BOSS/THREAT]. Arena: [ARENA]. Mood: [MOOD]. THINK FIRST (silently): the scale contrast that makes the boss feel overwhelming, the staging that puts the small hero in the foreground and the looming threat beyond, the reading order and focal flow, the atmosphere and light that crank the tension, and the one frozen beat that captures the stakes. Then write ONE copy-paste image description in flowing sentences covering, in order: the hero small in the foreground in a defiant ready pose, the towering boss or threat dominating the mid-ground with a clear menacing silhouette, the arena environment establishing place and danger, dramatic scale cues and depth, atmosphere (smoke, embers, dust, debris in motion), lighting (high-contrast key + colored rim, motivated by the threat itself, named color temp), camera (low wide heroic angle that exaggerates scale, lens), art direction (epic cinematic concept art, painterly realism), and mood. HARD RULES: clear focal hierarchy hero-then-boss, consistent perspective and light, anatomically coherent figures, no text. End with a Midjourney param line: --ar 21:9 --v 6 --style raw --s 280. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <the full description + param line> 2) STAGING NOTE: <one line on how scale and composition sell the stakes> 3) PHASE SWAP: <one phrase that escalates the boss into a more dangerous form>. Nothing else.
